title: Flamed Brazilian Farfalle Pasta
description: A charred, creamy farfalle pasta infused with smoky Brazilian flavors, perfect for a cozy date night.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook 250g gluten-free farfalle until al dente, about 8 minutes; drain and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add diced cherry tomatoes, diced red bell pepper, and diced red onion to the skillet and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until vegetables start to char around the edges.
Sprinkle 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1 teaspoon ground cumin, and 1 teaspoon ground coriander over the vegetables and cook for an additional minute to toast the spices.
Pour in 200ml coconut milk, add 1 tablespoon lime juice, and stir to combine; bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it thicken slightly, about 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and fold in the cooked farfalle, ensuring each noodle is coated with the creamy sauce, cooking for 2 minutes so the flavors mingle.
Transfer the pasta and sauce mixture to the prepared baking sheet, sprinkling 1 teaspoon sea salt over the top for a subtle crust.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes until the surface is lightly charred and bubbles form, creating a caramelized finish.
Remove from oven, drizzle with a squeeze of fresh lime zest, toss the pasta to distribute the zest, and serve hot for a date night delight.
